name: (gross-a) h2h GROSS — clean win. Subject A (Rick) gross 75 beats Subject B (Ben) gross 80; Ben's stakeholder pays Rick's the full stake.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the h2h engine on a GROSS basis (Story 1.3, FR13). h2h is winner-take-stake (the loser's stakeholder pays the winner's the full stake ONCE, NOT margin × stake) — identical to the net case; only the per-hole VALUES differ (gross strokes, not net). The engine is basis-agnostic and CONSUMES the per-hole values as a GIVEN input (P2 — it never re-derives them; the query layer feeds gross from netForSegment's gross column on a gross bet). `netPerHoleBySubject` holds the gross strokes here (field name shared with the net fixtures). `expected` is hand-calculated truth the engine must match. SettlementEdge direction: fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId (debtor → creditor). Money in integer cents. For h2h, result.marginNet is the |gross-total difference| and subjectNetTotal holds each subject's GROSS total.
_handCalc: Rick gross total = three 5s (holes 1-3) + fifteen 4s (holes 4-18) = 15 + 60 = 75. Ben gross total = eight 5s (holes 1-8) + ten 4s (holes 9-18) = 40 + 40 = 80. Lower total wins: 75 < 80, so subject A (Rick) wins by a margin of 5 gross strokes. h2h is winner-take-the-stake (not per-stroke): the LOSER's stakeholder (Ben) pays the WINNER's stakeholder (Rick) the full $25.00 = 2500 cents. Exactly one SettlementEdge: ben -> rick, 2500c.
